success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all expected nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-mode adapter was feasible (confidence 0.95) and aligns with UFL’s Python package architecture.
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio and passed plugin-level health checks.
Low intrusiveness risk and medium complexity made automated wrapping practical.
Core UFL APIs and algorithms were correctly identified and exposed as MCP endpoints.
failure_reasons: Validation gap: original project tests were not executed successfully despite reported 100% coverage metadata being inconsistent (passed=false, execution_time=0, no test files).
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external architectural/context enrichment.
Quality telemetry is incomplete (LOC, file size, resource/performance metrics reported as zero/empty), limiting confidence in quality/perf conclusions.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; repository imported via zip fallback, which likely increased setup latency but ensured continuity.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully with strong structural extraction (packages, dependencies, risk model, core modules).
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully; MCP scaffold and adapter files were created and endpoint surface was generated.
node_performance.test_time: Plugin health test passed quickly; however, no meaningful endpoint smoke/integration/performance tests were executed.
